verses.latin_vulgate: Post hæc aperuit Job os suum, et maledixit diei suo,
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אַֽחֲרֵי־כֵ֗ן פָּתַ֤ח אִיּוֹב֙ אֶת־פִּ֔יהוּ וַיְקַלֵּ֖ל אֶת־יוֹמֽוֹ׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: After this, Job opened his mouth, and cursed his day,
verses.finnish_biblia: Sitte avasi Job suunsa ja kirosi päivänsä.
verses.king_james: After this opened Job his mouth, and cursed his day.

verses.latin_vulgate: et locutus est :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּ֥עַן אִיּ֗וֹב וַיֹּאמַֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Job vastasi ja sanoi:
verses.king_james: And Job spake, and said,

verses.latin_vulgate: Pereat dies in qua natus sum,  et nox in qua dictum est : Conceptus est homo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֹ֣אבַד י֭וֹם אִוָּ֣לֶד בּ֑וֹ וְהַלַּ֥יְלָה אָ֝מַ֗ר הֹ֣רָה גָֽבֶר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let the day perish wherein I was born, and the night in which it was said: A man child is conceived.
verses.finnish_biblia: Se päivä olkoon kadotettu, jona minä syntynyt olen, ja se yö, jona sanottiin: mies on siinnyt.
verses.king_james: Let the day perish wherein I was born, and the night in which it was said, There is a man child conceived.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dies ille vertatur in tenebras :  non requirat eum Deus desuper,  et non illustretur lumine.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַיּ֥וֹם הַה֗וּא יְֽהִ֫י־חֹ֥שֶׁךְ אַֽל־יִדְרְשֵׁ֣הוּ אֱל֣וֹהַּ מִמַּ֑עַל וְאַל־תּוֹפַ֖ע עָלָ֣יו נְהָרָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let that day be turned into darkness, let not God regard it from above, and let not the light shine upon it.
verses.finnish_biblia: Se päivä olkoon pimiä, ja älköön Jumala kysykö ylhäältä sen perään: älköön kirkkaus paistako hänen päällensä.
verses.king_james: Let that day be darkness; let not God regard it from above, neither let the light shine upon it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Obscurent eum tenebræ et umbra mortis ;  occupet eum caligo,  et involvatur amaritudine.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִגְאָלֻ֡הוּ חֹ֣שֶׁךְ וְ֭צַלְמָוֶת תִּשְׁכׇּן־עָלָ֣יו עֲנָנָ֑ה יְ֝בַעֲתֻ֗הוּ כִּֽמְרִ֥ירֵי יֽוֹם׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let darkness, and the shadow of death, cover it, let a mist overspread it, and let it be wrapped up in bitterness.
verses.finnish_biblia: Pimeys ja kuolon varjo peittäköön hänen, olkoon pilvi hänen päällänsä; ja musta päivän sumu tehkään hänen kauhiaksi.
verses.king_james: Let darkness and the shadow of death stain it; let a cloud dwell upon it; let the blackness of the day terrify it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Noctem illam tenebrosus turbo possideat ;  non computetur in diebus anni,  nec numeretur in mensibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַלַּ֥יְלָה הַהוּא֮ יִקָּחֵ֢ה֫וּ אֹ֥פֶל אַל־יִ֭חַדְּ בִּימֵ֣י שָׁנָ֑ה בְּמִסְפַּ֥ר יְ֝רָחִ֗ים אַל־יָבֹֽא׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let a darksome whirlwind seize upon that night, let it not be counted in the days of the year, nor numbered in the months.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sen yön käsittäköön pimeys, ja älkään iloitko vuosikausien päiväin seassa, ja älkään tulko kuukausien lukuun.
verses.king_james: As for that night, let darkness seize upon it; let it not be joined unto the days of the year, let it not come into the number of the months.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sit nox illa solitaria,  nec laude digna.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּ֤ה הַלַּ֣יְלָה הַ֭הוּא יְהִ֣י גַלְמ֑וּד אַל־תָּב֖וֹא רְנָנָ֣ה בֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let that night be solitary, and not worthy of praise.
verses.finnish_biblia: Katso, olkoon se yö yksinäinen ja älköön yhtäkään iloa tulko siihen.
verses.king_james: Lo, let that night be solitary, let no joyful voice come therein.

verses.latin_vulgate: Maledicant ei qui maledicunt diei,  qui parati sunt suscitare Leviathan.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יִקְּבֻ֥הוּ אֹֽרְרֵי־י֑וֹם הָ֝עֲתִידִ֗ים עֹרֵ֥ר לִוְיָתָֽן׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let them curse it who curse the day, who are ready to raise up a leviathan:
verses.finnish_biblia: Ne jotka päivää kiroovat, he kirotkoot sitä, ne jotka ovat valmiit herättämään Leviatania.
verses.king_james: Let them curse it that curse the day, who are ready to raise up their mourning.

verses.latin_vulgate: Obtenebrentur stellæ caligine ejus ;  expectet lucem, et non videat,  nec ortum surgentis auroræ.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יֶחְשְׁכוּ֮ כּוֹכְבֵ֢י נִ֫שְׁפּ֥וֹ יְקַו־לְא֥וֹר וָאַ֑יִן וְאַל־יִ֝רְאֶ֗ה בְּעַפְעַפֵּי־שָֽׁחַר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Let the stars be darkened with the mist thereof: let it expect light, and not see it, nor the rising of the dawning of the day:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sen tähdet olkoot pimiät hämärässänsä, odottakoot valkeutta, ja ei tulko, ja älkööt nähkö aamuruskon silmäripsiä,
verses.king_james: Let the stars of the twilight thereof be dark; let it look for light, but have none; neither let it see the dawning of the day:

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia non conclusit ostia ventris qui portavit me,  nec abstulit mala ab oculis meis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י לֹ֣א סָ֭גַר דַּלְתֵ֣י בִטְנִ֑י וַיַּסְתֵּ֥ר עָ֝מָ֗ל מֵעֵינָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Because it shut not up the doors of the womb that bore me, nor took away evils from my eyes.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ettei se sulkenut minun kohtuni ovea, ja ei kätkenyt onnettomuutta silmäini edestä.
verses.king_james: Because it shut not up the doors of my mother’s womb, nor hid sorrow from mine eyes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare non in vulva mortuus sum ?  egressus ex utero non statim perii ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָ֤מָּה לֹּ֣א מֵרֶ֣חֶם אָמ֑וּת מִבֶּ֖טֶן יָצָ֣אתִי וְאֶגְוָֽע׃
verses.douay_rheims: Why did I not die in the womb? why did I not perish when I came out of the belly?
verses.finnish_biblia: Miksi en minä kuollut äitini kohdussa? Miksi en minä läkähtynyt äitini kohdusta tultuani?
verses.king_james: Why died I not from the womb? why did I not give up the ghost when I came out of the belly?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare exceptus genibus ?  cur lactatus uberibus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַ֭דּוּעַ קִדְּמ֣וּנִי בִרְכָּ֑יִם וּמַה־שָּׁ֝דַ֗יִם כִּ֣י אִינָֽק׃
verses.douay_rheims: Why received upon the knees? why suckled at the breasts?
verses.finnish_biblia: Miksi he ovat ottaneet minun helmaansa? Miksi minä olen nisiä imenyt?
verses.king_james: Why did the knees prevent me? or why the breasts that I should suck?

verses.latin_vulgate: Nunc enim dormiens silerem,  et somno meo requiescerem
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־עַ֭תָּה שָׁכַ֣בְתִּי וְאֶשְׁק֑וֹט יָ֝שַׁ֗נְתִּי אָ֤ז ׀ יָנ֬וּחַֽ לִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: For now I should have been asleep and still, and should have rest in my sleep:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin minä nyt makaisin, olisin alallani, lepäisin, ja minulla olis lepo.
verses.king_james: For now should I have lain still and been quiet, I should have slept: then had I been at rest,

verses.latin_vulgate: cum regibus et consulibus terræ,  qui ædificant sibi solitudines ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עִם־מְ֭לָכִים וְיֹ֣עֲצֵי אָ֑רֶץ הַבֹּנִ֖ים חֳרָב֣וֹת לָֽמוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: With kings and consuls of the earth, who build themselves solitudes:
verses.finnish_biblia: Maan kuningasten ja neuvojain kanssa, jotka heillensä rakentavat sitä mikä kylmillä on;
verses.king_james: With kings and counsellors of the earth, which built desolate places for themselves;

verses.latin_vulgate: aut cum principibus qui possident aurum,  et replent domos suas argento ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: א֣וֹ עִם־שָׂ֭רִים זָהָ֣ב לָהֶ֑ם הַֽמְמַלְאִ֖ים בָּתֵּיהֶ֣ם כָּֽסֶף׃
verses.douay_rheims: Or with princes, that possess gold, and fill their houses with silver:
verses.finnish_biblia: Eli päämiesten kanssa, joilla kultaa on, ja joiden huoneet ovat täynnä hopiaa;
verses.king_james: Or with princes that had gold, who filled their houses with silver:

verses.latin_vulgate: aut sicut abortivum absconditum non subsisterem,  vel qui concepti non viderunt lucem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: א֤וֹ כְנֵ֣פֶל טָ֭מוּן לֹ֣א אֶהְיֶ֑ה כְּ֝עֹלְלִ֗ים לֹא־רָ֥אוּ אֽוֹר׃
verses.douay_rheims: Or as a hidden untimely birth, I should not be; or as they that, being conceived, have not seen the light.
verses.finnish_biblia: Eli niinkuin keskensyntyneet kätketyt; ja en oliskaan: niinkuin nuoret lapset, jotka ei koskaan valkeutta nähneet.
verses.king_james: Or as an hidden untimely birth I had not been; as infants which never saw light.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ibi impii cessaverunt a tumultu,  et ibi requieverunt fessi robore.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שָׁ֣ם רְ֭שָׁעִים חָ֣דְלוּ רֹ֑גֶז וְשָׁ֥ם יָ֝נ֗וּחוּ יְגִ֣יעֵי כֹֽחַ׃
verses.douay_rheims: There the wicked cease from tumult, and there the wearied in strength are at rest.
verses.finnish_biblia: Siellä täytyy jumalattomain lakata väkivallastansa; siellä ovat ne levossa, jotka paljon vaivaa nähneet ovat;
verses.king_james: There the wicked cease from troubling; and there the weary be at rest.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et quondam vincti pariter sine molestia,  non audierunt vocem exactoris.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: יַ֭חַד אֲסִירִ֣ים שַׁאֲנָ֑נוּ לֹ֥א שָׁ֝מְע֗וּ ק֣וֹל נֹגֵֽשׂ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they sometime bound together without disquiet, have not heard the voice of the oppressor.
verses.finnish_biblia: Siellä on vangeilla rauha muiden kanssa, ja ei kuule vaatian ääntä;
verses.king_james: There the prisoners rest together; they hear not the voice of the oppressor.

verses.latin_vulgate: Parvus et magnus ibi sunt,  et servus liber a domino suo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: קָטֹ֣ן וְ֭גָדוֹל שָׁ֣ם ה֑וּא וְ֝עֶ֗בֶד חׇפְשִׁ֥י מֵאֲדֹנָֽיו׃
verses.douay_rheims: The small and great are there, and the servant is free from his master.
verses.finnish_biblia: Siellä ovat sekä pienet että suuret, ja palveliat vapaat isännistänsä:
verses.king_james: The small and great are there; and the servant is free from his master.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quare misero data est lux,  et vita his qui in amaritudine animæ sunt :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָ֤מָּה יִתֵּ֣ן לְעָמֵ֣ל א֑וֹר וְ֝חַיִּ֗ים לְמָ֣רֵי נָֽפֶשׁ׃
verses.douay_rheims: Why is light given to him that is in misery, and life to them that are in bitterness of soul?
verses.finnish_biblia: Miksi valkeus on annettu vaivaisille, ja elämä murheellisille sydämille?
verses.king_james: Wherefore is light given to him that is in misery, and life unto the bitter in soul;

verses.latin_vulgate: qui expectant mortem, et non venit,  quasi effodientes thesaurum ;
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַֽמְחַכִּ֣ים לַמָּ֣וֶת וְאֵינֶ֑נּוּ וַֽ֝יַּחְפְּרֻ֗הוּ מִמַּטְמוֹנִֽים׃
verses.douay_rheims: That look for death, and it cometh not, as they that dig for a treasure:
verses.finnish_biblia: Niille, jotka odottavat kuolemaa, ja ei se tule, ja kaivaisivat sitä ennen kuin aarnihautaa?
verses.king_james: Which long for death, but it cometh not; and dig for it more than for hid treasures;

verses.latin_vulgate: gaudentque vehementer  cum invenerint sepulchrum ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הַשְּׂמֵחִ֥ים אֱלֵי־גִ֑יל יָ֝שִׂ֗ישׂוּ כִּ֣י יִמְצְאוּ־קָֽבֶר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And they rejoice exceedingly when they have found the grave?
verses.finnish_biblia: Niille, jotka kovin iloitsevat ja riemuitsevat, että he saisivat haudan?
verses.king_james: Which rejoice exceedingly, and are glad, when they can find the grave?

verses.latin_vulgate: viro cujus abscondita est via  et circumdedit eum Deus tenebris ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְ֭גֶבֶר אֲשֶׁר־דַּרְכּ֣וֹ נִסְתָּ֑רָה וַיָּ֖סֶךְ אֱל֣וֹהַּ בַּעֲדֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: To a man whose way is hidden, and God hath surrounded him with darkness?
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja sille miehelle, jonka tie kätketty on, ja hänen edestänsä Jumalalta peitetty?
verses.king_james: Why is light given to a man whose way is hid, and whom God hath hedged in?

verses.latin_vulgate: Antequam comedam, suspiro ;  et tamquam inundantes aquæ, sic rugitus meus :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־לִפְנֵ֣י לַ֭חְמִי אַנְחָתִ֣י תָבֹ֑א וַֽיִּתְּכ֥וּ כַ֝מַּ֗יִם שַׁאֲגֹתָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Before I eat I sigh: and as overflowing waters, so is my roaring:
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä minun leipäni tykönä minä huokaan, ja minun parkuni vuodatetaan niinkuin vesi,
verses.king_james: For my sighing cometh before I eat, and my roarings are poured out like the waters.

verses.latin_vulgate: quia timor quem timebam evenit mihi,  et quod verebar accidit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֤י פַ֣חַד פָּ֭חַדְתִּי וַיֶּאֱתָיֵ֑נִי וַאֲשֶׁ֥ר יָ֝גֹ֗רְתִּי יָ֣בֹא לִֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: For the fear which I feared, hath come upon me: and that which I was afraid of, hath befallen me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Sillä jota minä pelkäsin, se tuli minun päälleni, ja mitä minä kartin, tapahtui minulle.
verses.king_james: For the thing which I greatly feared is come upon me, and that which I was afraid of is come unto me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nonne dissimulavi ? nonne silui ? nonne quievi ?  et venit super me indignatio.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֹ֤א שָׁלַ֨וְתִּי ׀ וְלֹ֖א שָׁקַ֥טְתִּי וְֽלֹא־נָ֗חְתִּי וַיָּ֥בֹא רֹֽגֶז׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: Have I not dissembled? have I not kept silence? have I not been quiet? and indignation is come upon me.
verses.finnish_biblia: Enkö minä ollut onnellinen? enkö minä ollut rauhassa? eikö minulla ollut hyvä lepo? ja nyt senkaltainen levottomuus tulee.
verses.king_james: I was not in safety, neither had I rest, neither was I quiet; yet trouble came.
